text: @eternalemerald The reason is to keep my skills getting better. Before I got a job, I was getting better every single day, and that stopped for a couple of months when I started working professionally. I wanted to keep getting better and better, and I have actually improved a great deal just over these last 9 dances :) Also, I want to give back to the Valve community that has given me so much.

text:
@thejazzman9475 
HI, I'm also a games animator and I was so blown away by your comment that you've only been animating since last March! Did you just teach yourself, Did u read animation books or did you go to school? Either way incredible work!
author: @svrack

text: @Abeguy2 I've been animating since last March, there is no secret to the movement, I just do what feels right and make sure I know what each part of the body is trying to do.
author: @thejazzman9475

text: @Abeguy2 Grab gcfscape and get the textures from you gcf packs in steam folder. You will need vtf edit to open them, and the export them as a format your program understands.
author: @thejazzman9475

text: FYI, your vid was on the latest episode of Inside Gaming from Machinima.  Great job.  Can't wait to see the finished product.
